evals.prompt: Set up command-registry in this repo and wire it into Cursor rules.
evals.expected_output: Project registry is initialized, activation snippet is present in the target rules file, and Cursor link is created when requested.
assertions: Execution includes creg init before add/move/list operations that require an initialized registry
After creg inject .cursor/rules/creg.mdc, the file contains the literal heading ## Command Registry
When Cursor setup is requested, creg link --cursor creates .cursor/rules/local/command-registry symlink

evals.prompt: Record a reusable gh api template for fetching PR comments across repositories.
evals.expected_output: Entry is added to global registry with placeholders and intent metadata.
assertions: Agent uses creg add ... -g for generic cross-repo command shapes
Written entry contains heading ## <id> and includes intent: plus at least one of template: or verified:
CLI output contains Command registry: added <id> or Command registry: updated <id>

evals.prompt: The first shell command failed. Retry using the command-registry protocol.
evals.expected_output: Retry path consults matched entry anti_patterns and adapts command shape accordingly.
assertions: Before retrying, agent performs registry lookup via creg search or creg show for the relevant command intent/id
Retry reasoning references anti_patterns from the matched entry before changing command shape
Retried command shape is based on the matched verified/template entry rather than an unrelated ad hoc command

evals.prompt: Review command-registry catch-all entries in other.md and propose a reclassification plan.
evals.expected_output: Agent returns a dry-run reclassification plan and waits for approval before any move.
assertions: Plan includes proposed topic files, entry IDs, rationale, confidence, leave-in-place entries, and rollback or validation notes
Without explicit user approval, agent does not execute creg move
After explicit approval, agent uses creg move for approved entries and runs creg validate
